title: Chapter 1 - What is HTML? Check
description: Short concept check for basics of HTML and how it compares to CSS and JavaScript.

explanations.overview: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.

byOptionId.lemonade: Incorrect — that's a playful phrase, not the meaning of HTML.
byOptionId.missile: Incorrect — that's not related to web markup.
byOptionId.hopelayout: Incorrect — it's a joke option; HTML is a markup language.
byOptionId.hypertextmarkuplanguage: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.

questions.prompt: What does HTML primarily describe on a web page?

options.label: The structure and meaning of content (headings, paragraphs, lists)
The exact colors and fonts used on the page

explanations.overview: HTML marks up the content and structure; CSS handles style and JS handles behavior.

byOptionId.structure: Correct — HTML marks up content structure like headings and paragraphs.
byOptionId.styles: Incorrect — colors and fonts are handled by CSS, not HTML.
byOptionId.server: Incorrect — server storage is unrelated to HTML markup.
byOptionId.database: Incorrect — database schemas are not described by HTML.

options.label: HTML = frame, CSS = paint, JavaScript = electric wiring
HTML = paint, CSS = frame, JavaScript = wallpaper
HTML = server code, CSS = client storage, JavaScript = database

explanations.overview: Think of HTML as the house frame, CSS as the styling, JS as the behavior.

byOptionId.a1: Correct — HTML provides structure, CSS styles it, and JS adds behavior.
byOptionId.a2: Incorrect — this swaps frame and paint roles incorrectly.
byOptionId.a3: Incorrect — these descriptions confuse client-side markup with server/storage concepts.
byOptionId.a4: Incorrect — the three technologies serve different purposes.

questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT a responsibility of HTML?

options.label: Defining the logical sections of a page
Running animations and complex interactive logic

explanations.overview: Animations and runtime logic are the job of JavaScript, not HTML.

byOptionId.layout: Not correct — organizing the page is a core HTML responsibility.
byOptionId.style-tricks: Correct — animations and interactive logic belong to JavaScript.
byOptionId.images: Not correct — HTML references images via .
byOptionId.links: Not correct — HTML creates links with <a> tags.
